summary: setup.py clones unpinned mutable branch from a personal fork at wheel-build time
details: During PEP 517 wheel build (e.g. `pip install indextts-cli` resolving to the sdist), setup.py executes `git clone --depth 1 --branch dev-3.12 https://github.com/gabry-lab/index-tts` and copies the cloned tree into `src/indextts/`, where it is packaged into the installed wheel and imported as `indextts`. Two structural problems: (1) the ref `dev-3.12` is a mutable branch rather than a pinned commit SHA, so whoever controls that branch HEAD at install time ships arbitrary Python into installers — there is no way for an installer to audit or reproduce the bytes that end up on disk; (2) the cloned repository is a personal-account fork (`gabry-lab/index-tts`) while the package's own README advertises the upstream project (`index-tts/index-tts`) as the source. The README/setup.py provenance mismatch hides the fact that installation pulls code from a third-party account rather than the upstream maintainers. The fetched code is then incorporated into the installed package and runs whenever the installer imports `indextts`, giving the fork's owner persistent code-execution surface on installer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
